hints.text: Plates long **white front AND back** + blue strip left

shared_with.note: UK = white front but **yellow back**, mostly without the strip.

hints.text: **Yellow dashed outer road lines** — very common (essentially never in the UK)

shared_with.diff: NZ = Pacific vegetation + different signage; Ireland = hedgerows + Irish bilingual signs.

hints.text: **Only European country with yellow diamond warning signs** (regulatory signs stay red-border/white)
Signage **bilingual English + Irish (italics)** — places, words and phrases translated
Chevrons **yellow-on-black** (UK: white); yield sign says **YIELD** (UK: GIVE WAY)
Pole warning sticker: **lightning bolt but NO man being zapped** + often a second outlined rectangular sticker (UK sticker shows the man)
**Green-white bollards** (not fully unique but great in UK 50-50s); rural gates w/ **rounded corners** (UK: square — but N. Ireland also rounded!)
**Boreens** — tiny rural roads w/ a grass strip down the middle (strong in UK 50-50s); drives **left**; smallcam Gen4
**Cream-white buildings w/ black roofs**, sometimes grey corner stones; row buildings in cities (British brick still occurs)

Very green agricultural land + **hedgerows** — often indistinguishable from the UK; wooden poles w/ **pole steps** + occasional crossbars (also UK/SE/FI); **double yellow no-parking lines** (also UK/Malta); B&W poles w/ yellow bulb at crossings (like UK)

shared_with.diff: Decide via: chevron colour, YIELD, plate backs, pole stickers, gate corners, diamond signs.

hints.text: Regional roads 3-digit: **first digit = region, anti-clockwise from Dublin** (8/9 = newer, non-regional; ~half of the 8s = Dublin metro); **area codes grouped by first digit**

**West coast**: rugged hills, wild brown-yellow moorland, exposed rock (vs neat green east); **Donegal**: tiny hilly rural roads w/ Gen4; **Wicklow Mountains** (S of Dublin) look western — but never a coast view + Gen4 small roads
**EV Gen4 car** (grey back w/ black semicircle) → most of the east + parts of the NW (Dublin also has the normal blue car)
**Tabletop mountains** → N-Sligo/Leitrim; **Burren NP** = grey limestone karst; **Cork** = the hilly big city
